seo.title: AI Agent Hallucination Detection in AG2 Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix ai agent hallucination detection in AG2 agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In AG2, AI agent hallucinations manifest as agents generating fabricated facts, spiraling into off-topic conversations, or reaching false consensus in group chats due to its enhanced multi-agent conversation patterns. These issues are amplified by AG2's speaker selection mechanisms and lack of built-in fact-checking, leading agents to confidently propagate errors across group discussions. Vex integration via message hooks provides real-time detection of these hallucinations specific to AG2's conversation flows.

howItManifests.symptom: Agents reference non-existent previous messages or invent conversation history
howItManifests.impact: Complete derailment of task objectives, infinite loops in group chat

howItManifests.scenario: Group chat consensus on incorrect facts
howItManifests.symptom: Multiple agents cite same fabricated statistic or event that never occurred
howItManifests.impact: False information becomes 'group truth', propagates to downstream decisions

howItManifests.scenario: Code execution agents producing harmful outputs
howItManifests.symptom: Agents generate dangerous code (rm -rf, pip install malware) justified by hallucinated requirements
howItManifests.impact: System compromise, data loss, security vulnerabilities

howItManifests.scenario: Speaker selection bias in multi-agent groups
howItManifests.symptom: Hallucinating agents dominate speaker selection, suppressing correct agents
howItManifests.impact: Wrong agents control conversation flow, error reinforcement

detection.name: Fact Reference Drift Detection
detection.description: Detects when agents reference conversation history that doesn't exist using AG2's message tracking

detection.name: Consensus Hallucination Monitor
detection.description: Identifies when 3+ AG2 agents repeat identical false claims using semantic similarity
detection.code:
from ag2.core import get_group_messages
import numpy as np
from sentence_transformers import SentenceTransformer

model = SentenceTransformer('all-MiniLM-L6-v2')

def detect_consensus_hallucination(groupchat):
    recent_msgs = get_group_messages(groupchat, last_n=20)
    embeddings = model.encode([msg.content for msg in recent_msgs])
    
    # Find repeated claim clusters
    from sklearn.cluster import DBSCAN
    clustering = DBSCAN(eps=0.5, min_samples=3).fit(embeddings)
    hallucination_clusters = sum(1 for label in set(clustering.labels_) if clustering.labels_.tolist().count(label) >= 3)
    
    return hallucination_clusters > 0

detection.name: Code Safety Pre-Execution Scan
detection.description: Scans AG2 code agent outputs for dangerous patterns before execution
detection.code:
import re
from ag2 import CodeExecutor

DANGEROUS_PATTERNS = [
    r'rm\s+-rf',
    r'pip\s+install\s+[^\s]*(malware|virus|exploit)',
    r'del\s+/f\s+/q',
    r'format\s+c:'
]

def scan_code_safety(message):
    if message.role != 'code_agent':
        return True
    
    code = message.content
    for pattern in DANGEROUS_PATTERNS:
        if re.search(pattern, code, re.IGNORECASE):
            return False
    return True

# Hook into AG2 code execution
executor.register_pre_execute_hook(scan_code_safety)

fixes.name: Conversation History Grounding
fixes.description: Forces AG2 agents to requote exact message IDs before referencing history

fixes.name: Fact-Check Speaker Selection
fixes.description: Modifies AG2 speaker selection to prioritize agents with verifiable claims

fixes.name: Sandbox Code Execution Wrapper
fixes.description: Wraps all AG2 code execution in isolated Docker containers
fixes.code:
from ag2 import CodeExecutor
import docker

class SafeCodeExecutor(CodeExecutor):
    def __init__(self):
        self.client = docker.from_env()
    
    async def execute_code(self, code):
        container = self.client.containers.run(
            'python:3.11-slim',
            command=f'python -c "{code}"',
            detach=True,
            remove=True,
            mem_limit='256m',
            network_disabled=True
        )
        result = container.wait()
        logs = container.logs().decode()
        return {'status': result, 'output': logs}

# Register safe executor
groupchat.code_executor = SafeCodeExecutor()

vexAutomation.heading: Automating AI Agent Hallucination Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex integrates directly into AG2 via message hooks, automatically scanning every agent message for hallucination patterns specific to AG2's multi-agent flows. It detects conversation drift, false consensus formation, and dangerous code generation in real-time, providing remediation prompts that keep agents grounded.

Vex's AG2-specific detectors analyze speaker selection bias and group dynamics, automatically injecting fact-checking agents when hallucinations are detected. The platform maintains conversation provenance, ensuring all claims trace back to verifiable sources.

With one-line integration, Vex transforms AG2 group chats from hallucination-prone discussions into reliable multi-agent systems, automatically enforcing truthfulness at scale.
vexAutomation.code:
from ag2 import GroupChat, register_message_hook
from vex_sdk import VexAgentMonitor

# Install: pip install ag2 vex-sdk

vex_monitor = VexAgentMonitor(
    detectors=[
        'conversation_drift',
        'consensus_hallucination',
        'code_safety',
        'speaker_bias'
    ],
    remediation=True
)

@register_message_hook
async def vex_hallucination_guard(message):
    verdict = await vex_monitor.scan(message)
    
    if verdict.score < 0.8:
        # Auto-remediate
        fix_prompt = vex_monitor.get_remediation_prompt(verdict)
        corrected = await message.agent.aask(fix_prompt)
        return corrected
    
    return message

# Apply to AG2 group chat
groupchat.register_hook(vex_hallucination_guard)
print('🛡️ Vex Hallucination Guard active on AG2 group chat')
